extraction.one_line_summary: MAHARIC Matej won a photo finish against ASGREEN Kasper at 49.1 km/h after O'CONNOR Ben's 400m move failed, ending the fifth-fastest Tour stage in history.
tactical_patterns: Breakaway refused to form until 117km remained despite 40km of attacking, revealing peloton uncertainty about GC threat assessment.
Nine-rider break with 117km gap shattered when POLLET's mechanical created chaos, showing fragility of small elite breaks.
Israel Premier-Tech placed four riders in 38-rider chase group but mechanical/crash attrition nullified numerical advantage completely.
O'CONNOR attacked at 400m despite not being fastest sprinter, forcing ASGREEN and MAHARIC to close rather than positioning.

rider_signals.MAHARIC Matej: positive: third stage win this Tour fighting for late Gino Mäder reveals exceptional form sustainability into week three
rider_signals.ASGREEN Kasper: positive: second consecutive day in winning position at ultra-high speeds confirms diesel engine strength in long breakaways
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: neutral: tactical awareness strong attacking at 400m but lacks pure speed to hold off faster finishers
rider_signals.POLLET Neils: negative: chain snap mechanical at critical breakaway formation moment eliminated from contention entirely
rider_signals.CLARK Simon: negative: cramp with 32.5km remaining after attacking shows inability to sustain efforts in fastest Tour stages
rider_signals.STRONG Corbin: negative: Israel's four-rider advantage disappeared when Strong crashed, exposing team's vulnerability to single-rider losses
rider_signals.YATES Adam: neutral: caught in early GC split but towed back without incident, revealing team cohesion but early positioning error
